caseResults.validationSummary: Search results show a Speaker product with title and category visible, and Add button is present. However, the product details page has not been opened (we are still on the search results page), and there is no confirmation that the item was added to the cart (cart still shows 0).
improvements: Fix product title selector: after navigating to the product details page, the locator 'h1, h2, [data-testid="product-title"]' resolves to the homepage hero heading instead of the product title. Replace with a more specific selector such as '[data-testid="product-title"], .product-title, h1.product-name' and add a waitForURL or waitForSelector guard to confirm the details page has fully loaded before asserting the title contains 'Speaker'.

Explicitly click the 'Details' button on the Speaker product card in search results before asserting product title and category. The current script is asserting the title on the search results page without navigating to the dedicated product details page. Add a step: await page.locator('text=Pulse Mini Speaker').locator('..').locator('button:has-text("Details"), a:has-text("Details")').click() and await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle') before any detail-page assertions.

Add a waitForURL assertion after clicking the product Details button to confirm navigation away from the search results page (e.g., await page.waitForURL(/\/product\/|detail|item/i, { timeout: 10000 })) before checking title and category, preventing assertions firing on the wrong page.

Fix the Add to Cart flow: the script must click 'Add to Cart' on the product details page, not on the search results card. After navigating to the details page, locate the Add to Cart button with a specific selector such as 'button:has-text("Add to Cart"), [data-testid="add-to-cart-btn"]' and click it, then assert cart count increments or a toast/confirmation message appears.

Add an explicit cart confirmation assertion after clicking Add to Cart: use await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="cart-count"], .cart-badge, #cart-item-count')).not.toHaveText('0', { timeout: 8000 }) or await expect(page.locator('text=added to cart, text=Added, .toast-success')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 8000 }) to verify the item was actually added.

Increase selector specificity for the product category on the details page: use '[data-testid="product-category"], .product-category, span.category-label' instead of relying on generic headings, and assert the text is non-empty with await expect(categoryLocator).not.toBeEmpty() after confirming the details page is loaded.

Add a fallback retry mechanism for the search-to-details navigation: if the first search result click does not trigger navigation (e.g., SPA route delay), add await page.waitForTimeout(1000) or poll for URL change with a custom retry loop before proceeding to detail-page assertions.

caseResults.validationSummary: Search results show a Speaker product with title and category visible on the card. However, the Details button has not been clicked to navigate to a product details view, so the product title and category on the details page cannot be confirmed. Additionally, no cart confirmation (badge update or toast) is visible, as Add to Cart has not been triggered.
improvements: Fix the search result interaction: instead of asserting that the 'Details' link contains text 'Speaker' (it only contains 'Details'), click the Details link using its data-testid attribute (e.g., page.locator('[data-testid^="view-product"]').first().click()) to navigate to the product details page before performing any title/category assertions

After clicking the Details link, wait for navigation to the product details page using page.waitForURL('**/#/product/**') or page.waitForLoadState('networkidle') before attempting to locate and assert the product title

Assert the product title on the details page using a heading or prominent element selector such as page.getByRole('heading').filter({ hasText: /speaker/i }) or page.locator('[data-testid*="product-title"]') rather than attempting to read text from the search result card link

Assert the product category on the details page by targeting a dedicated category element (e.g., page.locator('[data-testid*="category"], .product-category, .category-label').first()) and verify it is visible and has non-empty text using expect(locator).toBeVisible() and expect(locator).not.toBeEmpty()

After asserting title and category on the details page, locate the Add to Cart button using page.getByRole('button', { name: /add to cart/i }) and click it, then assert cart confirmation by checking for a toast/snackbar message or a cart badge count greater than 0 (e.g., expect(page.locator('[data-testid*="cart-badge"], .cart-count')).not.toHaveText('0'))

Remove the flawed expectTextWithFallback call that checks the Details link text for 'Speaker' — this assertion is logically incorrect since the link label is 'Details' by design; the product name should only be verified on the details page after navigation
Add explicit waits after each navigation step using page.waitForSelector() or expect(locator).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10000 }) to prevent premature assertions on elements that have not yet rendered
Capture a screenshot at each major step (after search results load, after details page loads, after Add to Cart) using testInfo.attach() to improve debugging visibility for future failures

caseResults.failureCause: Error: Unable to resolve locator. selector="[data-testid='product-category'], .product-category, text=/Category/i" description="Verify the product category element is visible on the product details page"
caseResults.validationSummary: The product details page shows 'Pulse Mini Speaker' with a valid category 'AUDIO' and an 'Add to cart' button. However, the search results page is not visible in the screenshot, and there is no confirmation message or cart icon update shown after adding to cart.
improvements: Fix the product category selector by inspecting the actual DOM on the product details page and replacing the failing candidates '[data-testid='product-category'], .product-category, text=/Category/i' with the confirmed selector that matches the 'AUDIO' label (e.g., '.product-category-label', '[class*="category"]', or a role-based locator like page.getByText('AUDIO') with a surrounding container check).

Add a DOM inspection step (page.content() or screenshot-on-failure with full-page) immediately before the category assertion to log available selectors and class names, enabling faster diagnosis of future selector mismatches.

Expand the category selector fallback list to include broader attribute patterns: '[class*="category"]', '[class*="Category"]', 'span:has-text(/AUDIO|Electronics|Audio/i)', and '[data-testid*="category"]' to increase resilience across product pages.

After clicking 'Add to Cart', add an explicit wait for a cart confirmation signal: either wait for cart count to change from 0 (e.g., await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="cart-count"], .cart-count, .cart-icon span')).not.toHaveText('0')) or wait for a toast/snackbar message matching /added to cart/i with a 5-second timeout before taking the final screenshot.

Capture a full-page screenshot at the search results stage (after pressing Enter and before clicking the first product) to validate the 'Search results page displays at least one product' criterion, which currently returns UNKNOWN due to missing screenshot evidence.

Refactor the resolveLocator function to log all attempted selectors and their visibility states to the test report when resolution fails, rather than throwing immediately, so partial matches are surfaced for debugging.

Add an explicit assertion on the cart item count or confirmation message using page.waitForSelector with a strict timeout (e.g., 5000ms) after the Add to Cart click, and fail with a descriptive message if neither a count increment nor a success toast is detected within the timeout.

Introduce a post-search assertion step that waits for at least one product card containing 'Speaker' in its title to be visible in the search results before proceeding, using locator('text=/Speaker/i').first() with a reasonable timeout to prevent silent navigation failures.

caseResults.validationSummary: Search results show a Speaker product with correct title and AUDIO category, but 'Add to Cart' has not been clicked yet, so no cart update or confirmation is visible. Cart still shows 0.
improvements: Fix the product title selector to target the product details page heading specifically rather than the generic 'h1, h2, .product-title, [class*="title"]' combo — the current selector resolves to the homepage hero h1 ('Shop your daily essentials with style.') instead of the product title. After navigating to the product details page, use a scoped selector such as page.locator('.product-detail h1, .product-name, [data-testid="product-title"], .product-info h1').first() and assert it contains 'Speaker'

Add an explicit waitForNavigation or waitForURL assertion after clicking the first Speaker search result to confirm the browser has fully transitioned to the product details page before asserting the product title — this prevents the title check running against the search results or homepage DOM

Ensure the 'Add to Cart' button click is actually executed in the test flow; based on the validation screenshot the button was visible but never clicked. Confirm the test script reaches and awaits page.locator('button:has-text("Add"), [data-testid="add-to-cart"], .add-to-cart-btn').click() and does not short-circuit due to the earlier title assertion failure

After clicking 'Add to Cart', add a dedicated assertion for cart confirmation: await expect(page.locator('.cart-count, [data-testid="cart-count"], .cart-badge')).not.toHaveText('0') OR await expect(page.locator('.toast, .snackbar, [role="alert"], [class*="confirmation"]')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 }) to validate the cart update criterion

Introduce a waitForSelector guard before asserting category — wait for page.locator('.category-label, [class*="category"], [data-testid="product-category"]').first() to be visible before asserting its text contains a valid category value, preventing race conditions on slower page loads

Scope all product detail assertions inside a page.waitForURL pattern or a landmark container selector (e.g., page.locator('.product-detail-page, [data-testid="product-detail"]')) so that title, category, and add-to-cart interactions are guaranteed to occur on the correct page context and not bleed into search result or hero banner elements

caseResults.validationSummary: The screenshot shows the home/search page with 'Speaker' entered in the search bar, displaying one result: 'Pulse Mini Speaker' with category 'AUDIO' visible. The product title contains 'Speaker' and the category is non-empty. However, the product details page has not been opened, 'Add to Cart' confirmation feedback is not shown, and the cart has not been verified to contain the Speaker item. The visible criteria pass, but flow-dependent criteria are unverifiable from this single screenshot.
improvements: Increase the Playwright test timeout from 60000ms to at least 120000ms globally and per test to prevent premature timeout failures during multi-step e2e flows involving navigation, search, and cart verification
Add explicit waitFor conditions after each navigation step (e.g., await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle') or waitForURL) to ensure pages are fully loaded before proceeding to next interactions

Replace or supplement the final screenshot with intermediate screenshots after each major step (search submission, product details page load, add-to-cart confirmation, cart page) to improve debugging visibility and validation coverage

After typing 'Speaker' in the search bar, explicitly wait for search results to appear using await page.waitForSelector('[data-testid="search-result"]') or equivalent before attempting to click a result

Click the 'Details' button on the search result card to navigate to the product details page, then assert the product title and category on that page specifically — the current test appears to stall before navigating off the search results page

Add a dedicated assertion step after clicking 'Details': await expect(page.locator('h1, [data-testid="product-title"]')).toContainText('Speaker') to confirm the details page loaded with the correct product

Add an explicit assertion for the product category on the details page using a targeted selector such as await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="product-category"]')).not.toBeEmpty() rather than relying on the search result card category label

After clicking 'Add to Cart', wait for confirmation feedback using await page.waitForSelector('[data-testid="cart-confirmation"], .toast, .modal, [aria-live]') with a reasonable timeout before proceeding to cart verification

Add a dedicated cart verification step: navigate to the cart page, then assert await expect(page.locator('.cart-item, [data-testid="cart-item"]')).toContainText('Speaker') to complete the full end-to-end flow

Wrap the screenshot calls in the finally block with a try-catch to prevent screenshot errors from masking the actual root cause of the test failure, and use page.isClosed() guard already present but ensure timeout does not propagate into finally

Refactor the test into clearly separated async helper functions (searchForProduct, openProductDetails, verifyTitleAndCategory, addToCart, verifyCart) to isolate failures at each step and improve maintainability
Consider using Playwright's expect with soft assertions for title and category checks so both can be validated even if one fails, ensuring full coverage reporting per acceptance criterion

caseResults.validationSummary: Search results show a Speaker product with visible title and category, but the screenshot does not show a product details page load nor a cart confirmation after adding to cart. The current view is the search results page, not the product details page.
improvements: Fix product title selector: The locator '[data-testid=\'product-title\'], .product-title, h1' is matching the homepage hero h1 ('Shop your daily essentials with style.') instead of the product name on the details page. After navigating to the product details page, use a more specific selector such as '[data-testid=\'product-name\'], .product-detail h1, .product-details-title, h1.product-title' and add a waitForURL or waitForSelector guard to confirm the details page has fully loaded before asserting the title.

Add explicit navigation to product details page: After clicking the first search result product card or 'Details' button, add 'await page.waitForURL(/.*product.*|.*detail.*|.*item.*/i)' or 'await page.waitForLoadState(\'domcontentloaded\')' to confirm the browser has actually navigated away from the search results page before attempting any assertions on the product details page.

Distinguish search result card selectors from product details page selectors: The test must not assert product title or category on the search results page. Scope all product detail assertions strictly to the product details page by waiting for a unique details-page element (e.g., 'Add to Cart' button or a product description section) to be visible before running title/category checks.

Fix 'Add to Cart' flow and post-action assertion: After clicking 'Add to Cart', explicitly wait for a cart confirmation signal using one of: 'await page.waitForSelector(\'.cart-notification, [data-testid=\'cart-success\'], .toast, [aria-live]\')' or assert the cart badge count increments from 0 to 1 via 'await expect(page.locator(\'.cart-count, [data-testid=\'cart-badge\']\')). not.toHaveText(\'0\')'.

Improve product click targeting on search results: Instead of relying on a generic first h1, locate the first product card containing 'Speaker' and click its dedicated details/view link using 'page.locator(\'.product-card:has-text(\'Speaker\')\').first().locator(\'a, button:has-text(\'Details\')\').click()' to ensure the correct product is opened.

Add screenshot checkpoints at each major step: Capture named screenshots after (1) search results load, (2) product details page load, and (3) add-to-cart confirmation. This will make future failures immediately diagnosable by the validation layer and reduce UNKNOWN outcomes in UI validation checks.

Increase assertion timeout for product details page load: Change the default 5000ms timeout on the title assertion to at least 10000ms using 'await expect(locator).toContainText(\'Speaker\', { timeout: 10000 })' to account for slower page transitions on the product details page.

caseResults.failureCause: Error: Unable to resolve locator. selector=".product-name, .product-details-title, [data-testid='product-name'], h2.product-title, .product-detail h2" description="Verify the product title on the details page contains the word 'Speaker'"
caseResults.validationSummary: The product details page shows 'Pulse Mini Speaker' with category 'AUDIO' and an Add to Cart button. The title contains 'Speaker' and the category is visible. However, the screenshot only shows the product details page — the search results page with a product card is not visible, and no cart confirmation signal (toast, notification, or updated cart badge count change) is shown after adding to cart. The cart badge still shows 0.
improvements: Replace the failed product title selector group '.product-name, .product-details-title, [data-testid='product-name'], h2.product-title, .product-detail h2' with a broader DOM inspection approach: after navigation to the product details page, use page.locator('h1, h2, h3').filter({ hasText: /speaker/i }).first() to dynamically match any heading containing 'Speaker', eliminating brittle class-name dependency

Add a page.waitForLoadState('networkidle') or page.waitForURL('**/product/**') call immediately after clicking the product link in search results, ensuring the product details page is fully loaded before attempting to resolve title/category locators

Implement a DOM snapshot fallback: if no candidate selector resolves within 3 seconds, call page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerHTML) and log the first 2000 characters to the error context file, enabling accurate selector discovery on failure without requiring a manual re-run

Fix the Add to Cart post-action assertion: after clicking 'Add to cart', poll for ANY of these signals with a 5-second timeout — (a) a toast/snackbar containing text like 'added', 'cart', or 'success' via page.locator('[class*=toast],[class*=snack],[class*=notification],[role=alert]'), (b) cart badge count changing from its initial value via expect(cartBadge).not.toHaveText('0'), or (c) a modal confirmation — and fail the test only if none of these appear

Capture a screenshot at each major step boundary (after search results load, after product details load, after Add to Cart click) using await page.screenshot({ path: `step-${stepNumber}.png` }) to give the validation agent full visual coverage of all acceptance criteria, resolving the UNKNOWN status on search results and navigation checks

Add an explicit assertion on the product category element using a resilient selector: page.locator('[class*=category],[class*=breadcrumb],[data-testid*=category]').or(page.locator('text=/AUDIO|Electronics|Speaker/i')).first(), then assert await expect(categoryLocator).toBeVisible() and await expect(categoryLocator).not.toHaveText('') to cover the non-empty category requirement

Before the search step, store the initial cart badge count with const initialCount = await page.locator('[class*=cart-count],[data-testid*=cart-badge],[class*=badge]').innerText().catch(() => '0'), then after Add to Cart assert the count has incremented, making the cart update verification deterministic and not reliant on transient toast visibility
